---
title: "Walter Smith"
---

## Walter Smith

### Description

publisher

### Biography

He ‘took the Mozleys’ business when John Mozley died . . . trained at Macmillans . . . a gentleman and pleasant to deal with in all ways’ (1882). He was associated with CMY 1874-1887.  He moved the office from 6 Paternoster Row to 34 King Street Covent Garden, in 1881.  The note (MS Ewelme Cottage Museum) accompanying payment for Annie Lush\'s article \'A Trip to Norfolk Island\' has the printed address in Paternoster Row crossed out and the new address written in;  it is dated 24 May 1881.

### Letters

- [To Frederick Orridge Macmillan, 1899](../letters/3391_To_Frederick_Orridge_Macmillan.html)
- Letter from Jan 15th 1891
- Letter from Feb 25th 1888	
- Letter from Jany 11th [1888]
- Letter from March 4th 1885
- Letter from Novr 12th 1884 
- Letter from July 12th [1884] 
- Letter from May 5th [1884] 
- Letter from Decr 9th 1882

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
